sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV hosts Oud-Heverlee Leuven at Stayen in a crucial Belgian Pro League clash. Sint-Truidense, sitting 3rd with 39 points, are in strong form with 3 wins in their last 5 matches, while Leuven, languishing in 14th with 20 points, have struggled, failing to win in their last 3 games. The Sofascore model heavily favors a home win with a 61% probability.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV have shown consistency, scoring in all of their last 5 matches and securing key victories against tough opponents like Standard Liège and Club Brugge. In contrast, Oud-Heverlee Leuven have managed just 1 win in their last 5, with a lack of attacking output evident in recent draws and losses. Their defensive frailty could be exposed again.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Sint-Truidense VV won 2-1 in November 2025, while Leuven edged a 3-2 thriller earlier in March 2025. Both matches saw goals from both teams, indicating a trend of open, competitive games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Sint-Truidense, Keisuke Goto is in fine form with 4 goals in his last 5 matches, while Ryotaro Ito’s creativity (51 key passes this season) will be crucial. Leuven’s hopes may rest on Sory Kaba, who has 4 goals this campaign, but their lack of in-form players in recent games is concerning.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Insight
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV boast a strong attack (30 goals scored, 4th in the league) and a solid defense (23 conceded, 3rd best), with high possession (53.55%) and pressing stats. Leuven, with only 19 goals scored and 29 conceded, struggle to convert chances and defend effectively, which could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sint-Truidense’s superior form, league position, and home advantage, alongside Leuven’s ongoing struggles, a home win seems likely. Expect Sint-Truidense to dominate possession and create more chances, potentially leading to a comfortable victory. Both teams scoring remains a possibility based on head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Sint-Truidense VV holds a narrow 1-0 lead over Oud-Heverlee Leuven, thanks to an early goal by Ilias Sebaoui in the 9th minute. The home side has dominated much of the possession and shown greater intent in the final third, while Leuven has struggled to convert their opportunities despite creating some chances. The match remains open, with both teams showing moments of promise but also areas of vulnerability.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Sint-Truidense VV has been the more composed side in the first half, maintaining a higher share of possession and frequently entering the opposition's final third. Their early goal has given them confidence, and they've managed to create a couple of significant scoring opportunities, though they missed one big chance. Defensively, they've been solid, winning a majority of duels and aerial battles, which has helped them control the game's tempo. Ilias Sebaoui has been a standout performer with his goal, while players like Rihito Yamamoto have contributed creatively with key passes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven finds themselves trailing at the break, unable to capitalize on the chances they've created. They've had a notable opportunity to score but missed a big chance and even hit the woodwork, indicating some frustration in their attack. Their defensive stats show they’ve struggled in duels, losing ground in both aerial and ground challenges. Discipline has also been an issue with a couple of yellow cards already picked up, which could impact their approach in the second half. Mathieu Maertens has been their most threatening player in attack, getting shots on target.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Sint-Truidense VV dominate the attacking momentum for the majority of the time, especially in the opening stages and mid-half periods, with consistent spells of pressure that led to their early goal. They maintained a strong presence in the opposition half, pushing forward with intent during several key phases. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, however, showed a shift in momentum towards the latter part of the half, mounting a series of attacking moves and gaining traction as the whistle approached. Their late surge suggests they could come out with renewed energy after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Sint-Truidense VV, maintaining their composure in possession and exploiting Leuven’s defensive lapses will be crucial. They’ll need to convert their entries into the penalty area into more clear-cut chances. On the other hand, Leuven must tighten up defensively, especially in duels, while capitalizing on any counter-attacking opportunities. Their late first-half momentum could be a sign of things to come if they can avoid further disciplinary issues. Both teams have shown they can create big chances, so finishing will likely decide the outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half prediction
halftimeSections.text: Given Sint-Truidense VV’s control in the first half and their ability to create chances, they are likely to hold onto their lead and possibly extend it. Oud-Heverlee Leuven will push for an equalizer, but their defensive frailties and lack of clinical finishing might hinder their comeback. Expect the home side to remain dominant in possession, while Leuven might rely on quick transitions to threaten. A second goal for Sint-Truidense seems probable if they maintain their intensity, with Ilias Sebaoui a potential danger man once again.
